subject: [COMMON] Update common Elasticsearch chart to use version 7.9.3
commit_message:
[COMMON] Update common Elasticsearch chart to use version 7.9.3

Fixes issue with pods IPs configuration when there are more than one IP address.
Elastic Search 7.9.3 was recommended by SDNR team, and is proven to properly deal with DS networking.

Issue-ID: SDNC-895
Signed-off-by: Grzegorz Lis <grzegorz.lis@nokia.com>
Change-Id: I89e01b85782c042d259e34774a56d6ff9eb7c8fb

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Looks good to me, but SDNC PTL/SDNR leaders need to have a look as well.
Added Alex, Martin and Dan Timoney to give their views.

Patch Set 1:

The Jira used in the Issue-ID is for dual stack support, which doesn't seem right.  Is this upgrade truly needed to support dual stack, or is this to mediate a security vulnerability?

In either case, this seems quite late for Guilin, unless it is needed to resolve a blocking issue.  I'm absolutely fine with this in Honolulu, though.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> The Jira used in the Issue-ID is for dual stack support, which doesn't seem right.  Is this upgrade truly needed to support dual stack, or is this to mediate a security vulnerability?
> 
> In either case, this seems quite late for Guilin, unless it is needed to resolve a blocking issue.  I'm absolutely fine with this in Honolulu, though.

Yes, this is to support dual-stack.
We checked previous version 7.6.x - dual stack is not supported properly. 
The proper support was brought with 7.9.x - see comments to SDNC-895.

We`ve tested in our envioronment using 7.9.x versions already in the past, it would be nice, if someone from SDNR team could try 7.9.x and confirm.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > The Jira used in the Issue-ID is for dual stack support, which doesn't seem right.  Is this upgrade truly needed to support dual stack, or is this to mediate a security vulnerability?
> > 
> > In either case, this seems quite late for Guilin, unless it is needed to resolve a blocking issue.  I'm absolutely fine with this in Honolulu, though.
> 
> Yes, this is to support dual-stack.
> We checked previous version 7.6.x - dual stack is not supported properly. 
> The proper support was brought with 7.9.x - see comments to SDNC-895.
> 
> We`ve tested in our envioronment using 7.9.x versions already in the past, it would be nice, if someone from SDNR team could try 7.9.x and confirm.

Thanks for the clarification.  I'm  a little uneasy about the timing (we're already past
the planned RC1 date), but I'm okay with this as long as Morgan is also okay.  Morgan - within SDNC, only SDN-R is using elasticsearch. I can't speak to whether any other projects are using this container, but from a purely SDN-C perspective, this seems low risk.

Gating shall help us to reduce the uneasiness feeling 😊
But as we do not have SDNR smoke tests, we will just check that the patch does not side effect issues.
it would be good to get the feedback from SDNR team but I do not see objections for upstream component update

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

The commit message is inadequate to the content of this patch.
It should be rather:
[COMMON] Update common Elasticsearch chart to use latest version

+ at least one line of description
 apart from that I'm ok with this patch

Patch Set 3:

This will not work with new 7.9.x.
In Guilin sdnrdb-init-job checks the version and will fail if >7.6.1. and fails in case of 7.9.3
Therfore released sdnc-image (2.0.3) needs to be corrected as well.

I would suggest:
- no change in Guilin branch (sdnc/oom)
- remove check of elastic db in sdnc-image 2.1.0-...(master/Honululu)  --> please raise Jira
- for testing purposes, use sdnc overrides
sdnc:
  enabled: true
  image: onap/sdnc-image:2.1.0-STAGING-latest
  elasticsearch:
    image: bitnami/elasticsearch:7.9.3
    master:
      image: bitnami/elasticsearch:7.9.3

OK, I think, we can postpone this to Honolulu...
I have created a JIRA in the meantime, as requested --> https://jira.onap.org/browse/SDNC-1411

Alex, would You be able to start working on this soon?
This will help us to validate these things really soon in Honolulu release, so we coudl continue working on Dual Stack, and potentially IPv6 in SDNR from the very beginning of Honolulu.

For the moment, I would put it as WIP, and we continue once we branch Guilin out in OOM.

@Alex Dehn - Would You be able to start working on this (removal of version check in SDNC/R)?
